summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the XML parsing functionality is vulnerable to XXE injection. A crafted XML file with an external entity declaration successfully reads local file content (/etc/passwd) when parsed by DOMDocument::loadXML() without entity protection.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should parse the malicious XML file containing an XXE payload, resolve the external entity reference to file:///etc/passwd, and embed the file content in the parsed XML structure.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction script successfully parsed the XXE payload and demonstrated that the XML parser resolves external entities without protection. The proof file /tmp/xxe_proof.txt contains content retrieved via the XXE vulnerability.
verification_method: The reproduction script parses a crafted XML file with an XXE payload (<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">) and checks whether the external entity is resolved. The presence of /etc/passwd content in the parsed output confirms successful XXE exploitation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious XML file created with external entity: <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
XML parsed using DOMDocument::loadXML() without libxml_disable_entity_loader()
External entity resolved and file content embedded in XML structure
Proof file /tmp/xxe_proof.txt created with content from /etc/passwd
Confirmed absence of LIBXML_NOENT | LIBXML_DTDLOAD flags in parsing code

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the target repository from the session directory to a temporary workspace and mounts it into the container at /target-repo.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: 2.4.17 (latest version based on update directory structure)
